name: Kalamaria Parayemista
recipeInstructions:
1. Select squid with hoods about 10-12 cm (4-5 inches) long.
2. To clean squid, pull off the head and attached tentacles. Cut out the eyes and beak and discard them. Clean the dark skin from the head and tentacles by pulling it off or rubbing it off with a cloth dipped in coarse salt. Rinse thoroughly, chop into small pieces, and keep aside.
3. The remainder may be stored and fried later.
4. Clean out the hood (body) of the squid and remove the transparent backbone from inside. Pull or rub off the skin. Rinse well, drain, and dry.
5. Heat 1 tablespoon oil in a pan and gently fry the onion until transparent. Stir in rice and cook over heat for 2 minutes.
6. Add tomato puree, water, cinnamon stick, cloves, chopped squid, salt, and pepper to taste. Cover and simmer over low heat for 10 minutes or until the liquid is absorbed. Remove the cinnamon stick and cloves.
7. Fill hoods with the rice mixture, packing it in loosely as rice expands and hoods contract during cooking. Close the top with wooden cocktail picks or sew with strong thread.
8. Heat remaining oil in a deep pan and fry squid hoods until lightly colored, turning them frequently. Reduce heat, add wine, cover, and simmer gently on low heat for 1 hour or until the squid is tender. Add a little water to the pan if necessary during cooking. Serve hot or warm as an appetizer.
